titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Bertram Cubitt, 10 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is letter from Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary for Ireland, to the secretary of the War Office, Bertram Cubitt (1862-1942). The letter regard the importation of masonic swords into Ireland by George Kenning and Son, a masonic clothing and item distributor based in London. Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926) served as the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5612/9542/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10th June 1916. 9542. Sir, Referring to your letter of the 3rd instant
(Contracts.Firms.K/1739: A.G.3.) enquiring whether
there is any objection to Masonic Swords being
supplied by Messrs George Kenning & Son of 1, 2, &
3 Little Britain Street, London, E.C., to various
parts of Ireland, I am directed by the Lords Justices
to acquaint you, for the information of the Secretary
of State, that the General Officer Commanding-in-Chief
the Forces in Ireland, to whom the matter was re-
ferred, states that he has no objection to the
importation of a few swords fit for ceremonial
purposes only but that the permission of the Military
Authorities must first be obtained. Messrs Kenning & Son should therefore apply to
the Head Quarters, Irish Command, Park Gate, Dublin,
stating the number of swords and names of consignees,
when enquiries will be made, and if the Military
Authorities are satisfied as to the good character
and loyalty of the consignees, Permits will be issued. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant , E. O F. The Secretary, War Office, London, S.W.
